nodes.label: Functions Over Agents Principle
nodes.rationale: Fetch, noise-filter, classify, draft, and send are deterministic steps (plain functions or single LLM calls); agent loops and tool routers are forbidden for them because the only real agent needed is the read-only Chat Agent.

nodes.label: Single pgvector Store in Supabase
nodes.rationale: Never add a separate vector database (Pinecone, Weaviate); one embeddings table tagged by kind inside the existing Postgres keeps the stack simple, and a kind is only embedded once a concrete feature reads it.

nodes.label: Approval-Gated Outbound Policy
nodes.rationale: Every reply or post passes through the approval layer; policy auto-sends low-risk/low-value and hard-gates high-value or churn-risk so nothing is ever sent directly from a classifier, drafter, or the Chat Agent.

nodes.label: No Fine-Tuning Principle
nodes.rationale: Fine-tuning is explicitly deferred until there is labelled data and a measured quality ceiling; system prompts + few-shot examples + retrieval are used instead.

nodes.label: Additive Numbered Migrations
nodes.rationale: Never rewrite or destructively alter an existing migration; add a new sequentially-named file, and new tables get RLS from day one.

nodes.label: Tenant Isolation Everywhere
nodes.rationale: Every row carries team_id/workspace_id, every API mutation calls requireApiTenantContext(), and every new table enables tenant-scoped RLS.

nodes.label: iCloud Duplicate File Hazard
nodes.rationale: The repo is iCloud-synced and contains stale conflicted duplicates named '* 2.ext'; the canonical file is always the one without ' 2', so duplicates must never be read, edited, or imported.

nodes.label: Zero-Cost Noise Filter
nodes.rationale: String-based pre-filter runs before any paid AI call to eliminate wasted OpenAI spend on newsletters, auto-replies, and pleasantries; API cost per message drops 40-70% versus unfiltered.

nodes.label: Classification Prompt (GPT-4o)
nodes.rationale: Emits one strict JSON object (intent_type, urgency, sentiment, lead_score, revenue_risk, needs_human_approval, etc.) because the output is consumed directly by n8n automation; complaints and churn_risk default to needs_human_approval true, and vague messages lower confidence and favor human review.

nodes.label: Reply Generation Prompt (GPT-4o)
nodes.rationale: Drafts stay under 120 words with fixed entry pricing floors and no discounts/overpromises; approval_required must be true for anger, churn, refunds, custom pricing, or when classification_result.needs_human_approval is true, so risky sends always route to the owner queue.

nodes.label: Buy-Back Report Prompt
nodes.rationale: Turns one metrics JSON payload into a fixed-section markdown briefing under 500 words; every number must be grounded in the payload (missing data yields exactly 'Not enough data yet.') to prevent fabricated metrics in founder-facing reports.

nodes.label: Chat Agent v1 (Structured-Data, Read-Only)
nodes.rationale: No business documents exist in beta (confirmed with product owner), so Chat Agent v1 answers from structured tenant data (leads, conversations, reply_drafts, daily_reports, business_profiles) via read-only SQL instead of pgvector/document RAG; its suggestions go into the approval queue and it never sends anything itself.

nodes.label: Durable inbound_events Ledger + Idempotency
nodes.rationale: Replaces the in-memory dedup Map (lost on serverless cold start) and fire-and-forget n8n forward (silent message loss when n8n is down) with persist-before-ack: write the raw event to a durable table keyed on platform + message_id, return 200, then process. A drain/replay worker is still required before production launch since Meta will not redeliver after the 200.

nodes.label: Edge Tenant Resolution
nodes.rationale: Tenant is resolved at the Next.js webhook edge rather than in n8n so the inbound_events ledger is tenant-stamped at write time and routing lives in one typed place (lib/meta-tenant.ts).

nodes.label: 60-90 Day Historical Backfill on Connect
nodes.rationale: Run a historical backfill when Gmail/Meta connects so the dashboard is never empty and shows real numbers immediately.

nodes.label: Knowledge Layer (pgvector embeddings) — Deferred
nodes.rationale: The embeddings/pgvector store is deferred until customers actually upload documents — it must not be built speculatively.

nodes.label: Classification Prompt v1 Test Results (5/5 PASS)
nodes.norm_label: classification prompt v1 test results (5/5 pass)

nodes.label: Workstream 4: AI Image Generation (Hidden Prompt Enhancer)
nodes.rationale: The user never sees the enhanced prompt by default; a prompt-engineer step rewrites the raw prompt with style/composition/brand constraints, moderation runs, and generated media flows into the same publishing studio approval path.
nodes.norm_label: workstream 4: ai image generation (hidden prompt enhancer)

nodes.label: Workstream 5: Compliant Hiring Section
nodes.rationale: LinkedIn scraping and private profile harvesting are explicitly rejected: no public candidate-search APIs exist, Recruiter System Connect is contract-gated, and automation creates account-ban and legal risk. Hiring is built on owned/consented data (applicant forms, CV uploads, ATS integrations), and AI must not infer protected attributes.

nodes.label: Durable Idempotency & Queues Requirement
nodes.rationale: Fire-and-forget webhook forwarding can silently drop messages if n8n is down; payloads must be persisted or queued before returning 200, and dedupe must use durable keys (inbound: platform + message_id; outbound: reply_draft_id + platform).

nodes.label: Gmail Add-on / Metadata-First Flow
nodes.rationale: User-initiated, current-message-only ingestion via a Gmail add-on (POST /api/gmail/addon/ingest) replaces broad mailbox OAuth: narrower access, less token custody, and a far simpler Google review posture, while keeping WF0a as the unchanged downstream intake target.

nodes.label: Google Restricted Scope Problem (gmail.readonly)
nodes.rationale: gmail.readonly is a Google restricted scope requiring OAuth verification and CASA assessment; while in Testing mode only listed test users can connect (public users get 403 access_denied), Vercel preview URLs break registered redirect URIs, and storing long-lived refresh tokens for full-mailbox access enlarges the compliance surface.

nodes.label: n8n Validation Warning Impact Classification
nodes.rationale: Maps each n8n_validate_workflow warning to Blocker / Prod risk / Hygiene / False-positive; the one schema Blocker (conversations.source disallowing 'gmail') was fixed by migration 0004, applied on live Supabase in two execute_sql steps with an information_schema guard for the absent daily_reports.report_date column.

nodes.label: Meta Webhook (/api/meta/webhook)
nodes.rationale: Verifies hub.verify_token (GET) and X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC (POST) before trusting any payload, dedupes message ids, then forwards to the n8n gmail-inbound webhook where Tenant Route Extract resolves business_profiles by ig_account_id / fb_page_id / wa_phone_number_id / whatsapp_routing_number / webhook_route_token.

nodes.label: Tenant Route Resolution Order
nodes.rationale: Inbound items resolve the tenant in a fixed precedence: webhook token header, then WhatsApp routing number, then Gmail destination mailbox, with NEXUS_* env vars as fallbacks that must match business_profiles routing columns.
